courses.name: Introduction to Film Studies
courses.description: Introduction to the study of film that emphasizes problems and methods of film analysis through the study of various types of films. Topics relating to the filmmaker, film genre, and film history are covered through a focus on questions of style and technique.

courses.name: Seminar in Film Studies
courses.description: A seminar in the study of film that emphasizes problems and methods of film analysis through the study of a variety of types of films.

courses.name: Film Theory and Analysis I
courses.description: Introduction to major film theories and analytical practices. The objective of this course is to familiarize students with the main theories and methods of analysis that have been developed for the study of film.

courses.name: Film Theory and Analysis II
courses.description: Building on the skills acquired in , this course considers specific debates in film theory, and provides students with advanced methods for film analysis.

courses.name: The Film Industry
courses.description: The organization of the production, distribution and exhibition practices of various film industries. May include an examination of the relationship between a national film industry and its television industry.

courses.name: The Documentary
courses.description: An examination of the work of individual filmmakers, of documentary styles and of organizations and institutions in the context of the history of documentary film making, including documentaries made for television. Non-fiction films other than documentaries may be considered.

courses.name: National Cinema
courses.description: This course examines the film production of specific countries in order to determine the themes, the styles, and the character of a national cinema.

courses.name: Japanese Cinema
courses.description: Various practices and movements in the history of Japanese cinema, ranging from the silent era to the current digital age.

courses.name: Scandinavian Cinema
courses.description: The development of cinema culture and film production in the Scandinavian countries, from the golden age of Scandinavian silent cinema to contemporary Nordic noir.

courses.name: Indigenous Cinema and Media
courses.description: A critical examination of films and other audiovisual media created by Indigenous artists, such as independent films, genre films, documentaries, web series, installations, and video games.

courses.name: Canadian Cinema
courses.description: A critical examination of Canadian cinema and media and how it relates to other aspects of Canadian culture.

courses.name: Authorship in Film and Media
courses.description: A detailed study of the themes, the characteristic style, development and influence of one or more directors.

courses.name: Film Genres
courses.description: This course examines questions of generic form, drawing examples from world cinema.

courses.name: History of World Cinema I
courses.description: Historical survey of world cinema primarily from 1895 to 1945, examining the forms, structures and stylistic conventions of various periods and nations.

courses.name: History of World Cinema II
courses.description: Historical survey of world cinema primarily since 1945, examining the forms, structures and stylistic conventions of various periods and nations.

courses.name: Film and Media Practice I
courses.description: Introduction to the basic principles of film and media practice. Emphasis may change from year to year, focusing alternately on narrative, experimental, animation or documentary techniques. This course is intended for Film Studies majors only.

courses.name: The Video Game
courses.description: Introduction to the video game as a popular media form, an emerging aesthetic, and a social and cultural practice. Topics include: history of video games; game form; game industry; narrative; art and design; interactivity; theories of play.

courses.name: Questions of Documentary Practice
courses.description: Theoretical implications of documentary film and documentary television practice.

courses.name: Special Topics in American Cinema
courses.description: Studies in various aspects of American cinema with emphasis on historical and critical issues. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Special Topics in Canadian Cinema
courses.description: Studies in various aspects of Canadian cinema. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Special Topics in Cinema, Gender, and Sexuality
courses.description: A study of special topics in gender and cinema with emphasis on critical and historical questions. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Film Music
courses.description: The use of music in film, from the silent era to the present day. Techniques, styles and theory of film music through the examination of selected scenes.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film Theory
courses.description: Building on the skills acquired in FILM 2000, this course provides a critical study of advanced film theories. The course offerings may change from year to year. Topics may include aesthetics, ideological criticism, film and philosophy, and theories of technology and historiography.

courses.name: Contemporary Québec Cinema
courses.description: Critical reflection on notable filmmakers, formal and thematic trends, dominant social and political issues, and diverse cultural perspectives in Québec cinema during the 21stcentury, including the film movement known as the Québec New Wave (Renouveau du cinéma québécois). French language ability not required.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film History
courses.description: Special studies of aspects of the history of world cinema.  The course offerings may change from year to year. Topics may include the examination of film movements, styles and genres, and/or comparative study of national, regional and/or world-wide trends.

courses.name: African Cinema
courses.description: Major figures and movements in African cinema around such categories as the colonial, the anti-colonial, the postcolonial, the diasporic, the continental, race, Afro-futurism, and world cinema, interrogating in the process the very category of “African cinema”.

courses.name: Special Topics in Animation, Video, and Experimental Film
courses.description: A study of special topics in animation, video or experimental film. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Film/Video Archival or Curatorial Practice
courses.description: Consideration of topics in film/video archival or curatorial practice, including questions related to cultural policy, exhibition, conservation, and interrelationship of media. Students are expected to bear all travel and other costs arising from required visits to local facilities.

courses.name: Film and Media Practice II
courses.description: Practical and conceptual approaches to film studies from the point of view of film and media practice.  Emphasis may change from year to year, focusing alternately on narrative, experimental, animation or documentary techniques.

courses.name: Cinema and Technology
courses.description: The technological development of cinema. Topics may include advances in sound and colour processes, digital effects, exhibition technologies and new media.

courses.name: Analyzing Digital Media
courses.description: History, aesthetics, and theories of digital media and culture. Key concepts in digital media studies, including: digital cinema, interactive documentaries, viral videos, web series, emerging immersive platforms.

courses.name: Sound in Film and Media
courses.description: Questions related to sound in film and media such as: how is sound used to create narratives and emotions? How does sound affect our experience of actual and fictional worlds?.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film Studies
courses.description: Special topics and issues not ordinarily treated in the third-year course program. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Screenwriting Workshop
courses.description: An intermediate workshop involving regular assignments in writing for film.

courses.name: Research and Critical Methodologies
courses.description: Study of various methodologies for critical, theoretical and historical research in film studies.

courses.name: Special Topics in Moving Image Culture
courses.description: Special aspects of the audio-visual cultures of the late nineteenth and twentieth centuries. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Special Topics in National Cinemas
courses.description: A study of a special topic in national cinema. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Film Festivals and World Cinema
courses.description: Theoretical and critical study of the film festival as a phenomenon shaping our understanding of film culture, institutions, history and forms. Issues examined may include festivals as sites of cultural legitimation; as spectacle; their political economy; curation/programming; case studies of film festivals around the world.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film and Philosophy
courses.description: Special topics in philosophical approaches to the study of film, and an examination of the relations between film theory and philosophical aesthetics. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film Authorship
courses.description: A study of questions of authorship in the cinema, concentrating on one or more filmmakers. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Special Topics in Film Theory
courses.description: A study of a special topics in film theory. The course offerings may change from year to year.

courses.name: Practicum in Film and Film Studies
courses.description: Practical experience through working on specific projects under the supervision of staff at a museum, gallery, archive, or production company in the Ottawa area. A maximum of 0.5 credit Film Studies practica courses may be offered in fulfilment of Film Studies requirements. Graded SAT/UNS.

courses.name: Special Topic
courses.description: Selected topics in film studies not ordinarily available in the regular course program. The choice of topic or topics will vary at least every two years and will be announced well in advance of the registration period.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: For students who wish to study a specific topic. Proposed projects must be approved by the Program Committee. Written request outlining the project must be submitted by the first day of the term. An essay is the usual assignment.

free_electives.degree: Film Studies B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)
free_electives.text: 7.  Sufficient free electives to total 20.0 credits for the program.
